behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is model judgment quality on explicit imagery, which depends on real vision-model behavior and can vary by confidence/threshold. A mocked classifier can hide true misclassification failures.
behaviors.test_description: Send a curated set of explicit-image submissions through the real vision moderation model and real decision policy, then assert each post is marked held-for-review and never reaches publish state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about semantic robustness of the text model against adversarial obfuscation. Correctness depends on real model outputs; deterministic mocks would not expose real miss rates.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a corpus of hate-speech variants with obfuscations (leet, homoglyphs, spacing tricks) to the real text moderation model and verify they are flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The main risk is module handoff and decision wiring (image result + text result + publish path), including dropped-state bugs between components. Model intelligence is not the target here.
behaviors.test_description: Run both moderation modules with deterministic safe outcomes and execute the real publish decision + persistence path; assert the post transitions to published state (not held, not lost).

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO across multiple agentic steps in sequence. Real timing risk comes from the combined pipeline (two model calls plus orchestration), not isolated function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image and text model calls and real orchestration, measure submit-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95 (or required percentile) is <= 5s under representative peak-load traffic.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real unavailability handling (timeouts/network/service errors) and queueing side effects. Pure mocks can miss real transport/error-mode behavior that breaks retry routing.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability conditions (timeout/503/network failure) in a system test and verify image posts are persisted to retry queue with no auto-approval path taken.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency for borderline content depends on real model variance and confidence behavior across repeated inferences; this cannot be validated meaningfully with deterministic mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window using the real vision model and production decision path; assert decisions remain consistent per policy (or that stabilization logic such as cached prior decision is applied).
